{{Short description|none}} {{Use dmy dates|date=July 2020}} {{Infobox Film festival | name = 62nd Cannes Film Festival | image = 2009 Cannes Film Festival poster.jpg | caption = The poster shows a still from the film ''[[L'Avventura]]'' by [[Michelangelo Antonioni]].{{cite web|url=http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/theDailyArticle/56782.html |title=The Official 62nd Cannes Film Festival Poster |date=23 May 2009 |work=[[Cannes Film Festival]] |publisher=festival-cannes.com |access-date=24 May 2009 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20090620211829/http://www.festival-cannes.com/en/theDailyArticle/56782.html |archive-date=20 June 2009 |url-status=dead }} | opening = ''[[Up (2009 film)|Up]]'' | closing = ''[[Coco Chanel & Igor Stravinsky]]'' | location = [[Cannes]], [[France]] | founded = 1946 | awards = [[Palme d'Or]]:
''[[The White Ribbon]]'' | host = [[Édouard Baer]] | number = 20 (In Competition) | date = 13–24 May 2009 | website = {{URL|http://festival-cannes.com}} | main = [[Cannes Film Festival]] | previous = [[2008 Cannes Film Festival|2008]] | next = [[2010 Cannes Film Festival|2010]] }} The '''62nd Cannes Film Festival''' took place from 13 May to 24 May 2009. French actress [[Isabelle Huppert]] served as jury president for the main competition. Austrian filmmaker [[Michael Haneke]] won the [[Palme d'Or]], the festival's top prize, for the drama film ''[[The White Ribbon]]''.{{cite web|url=http://www.cinema-francais.fr/cannes/cannes_2009.htm |title=62ème Festival de Cannes |work=cinema-francais.fr |access-date=21 July 2017}} The festival opened with ''[[Up (2009 film)|Up]]'' by [[Pete Docter]],{{cite web|url=http://www.festival-cannes.com/en/films/up |title=Out of Competition - Opening Film - Up |work=festival-cannes.com |access-date=21 July 2017}}{{cite web|url=http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/article/56280.html |title=Up To Open Festival |access-date=24 April 2009 |work=festival-cannes.com |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120118033715/http://www.festival-cannes.com/en/article/56280.html |archive-date=18 January 2012 |url-status=dead }} marking the first time that an animated film opened the festival, and closed with ''[[Coco Chanel & Igor Stravinsky]]'' by [[Jan Kounen]].{{cite web|url=http://www.festival-cannes.com/en/films/coco-chanel-igor-stravinsky |title=Out of Competition - Closing Film - Coco Chanel & Igor Stravinsky |work=festival-cannes.com |access-date=21 July 2017}} American filmmaker [[Clint Eastwood]] became the second recipient of the [[Palme d'Or|Honorary Palme d'Or]].{{cite web|title=A Honorary Palme at the opening ceremony of the Festival de Cannes |url=http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/article/58029.html |access-date=3 September 2015 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160124000809/http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/article/58029.html |archive-date=24 January 2016 }} == Juries == [[File:Jury Cannes
